_metadata.description: Controlled vocabulary for the 7 genotoxicity classification values in efsa_openfoodtox_reference.json `genotoxicity` field. Reflects EFSA's risk-assessment outcome.
_metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for EFSA genotoxicity classification labels. Surfaced in tap-to-explain on the regulatory card.
_metadata.alignment: Pipeline source: scripts/data/efsa_openfoodtox_reference.json `genotoxicity` field (91 substances; live: negative=67, insufficient_data=8, indirect=7, positive=4, equivocal=3, cannot_be_excluded=1, under_review=1).

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: negative
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA assessment found no evidence of genotoxic effect. The most common classification.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: positive
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA assessment found evidence of genotoxic effect. Use is restricted or banned depending on the magnitude and pathway.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: equivocal
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA assessment was inconclusive — some assays positive, others negative. Treated cautiously pending follow-up data.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: insufficient data
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA could not reach a conclusion due to insufficient or low-quality data. Substance is treated cautiously by default.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: indirect
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA assessment found evidence of indirect genotoxicity (downstream pathway) rather than direct DNA damage. Mechanism-dependent risk.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: cannot be excluded
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA could not rule out a genotoxic effect from the available data. Default to precautionary handling.

genotoxicity_classifications.name: Genotoxicity: under review
genotoxicity_classifications.notes: EFSA review of genotoxicity is ongoing; classification may change.
